name: Local Worker Status
description: Diagnoses whether the local Hermes worker, Ollama runtime, Hermes Nous runtime, and local execution loop are online, reachable, and processing jobs.
purpose: Diagnose whether the local Hermes worker, Ollama runtime, and local execution loop are online, reachable, and processing jobs.
whenToUse: User says the local agent is not working or not functioning
User asks whether the local worker is online, offline, stale, or busy
User asks why a build did not run or did not route to the local worker
User reports jobs stuck, queue not processing, worker not claiming tasks, or no heartbeat
User asks about Ollama or local runtime reachability
User asks for safe diagnostic status of local execution without changing files
whenNotToUse: Do not use for general repo implementation when the worker is not the focus
Do not use for HR, I-9, job fit, resume, writing polish, email drafting, or calendar tasks
Do not use to start, stop, or mutate worker state without explicit user action
Do not call localhost from Vercel or the browser as a diagnostic shortcut
strongSignals: why did this not route to the local worker
requiredContext: Last claimed job and last completed job if available
Safe worker error, last fetch error, or known cloud/local boundary limitation
missingContextQuestions: Are you asking for worker status only, or should I also inspect why a specific build did not run?
Which task, project, or build request should I check if this is about one queued job?
Are you running the local worker on this machine with npm run worker:local?
safetyRules: Do not call localhost from Vercel directly
If the app cannot verify the local worker from cloud, say so clearly and instruct the user to run the worker locally
Never expose secrets, tokens, raw environment values, or sensitive logs
Do not start, stop, restart, or mutate jobs without an explicit user request and the existing execution path

outputContract.format: Worker status -> Queue status -> Last heartbeat -> Last job claimed -> Last job completed -> Ollama status if available -> Likely cause -> Next step.
mustAvoid: Expose secrets, auth tokens, raw env values, or private paths beyond safe labels
Claim the worker is online without heartbeat evidence
Mutate queue, files, or worker process state from a read-only diagnostic
Give direct implementation instructions that bypass approval gates

positiveExamples: My local agent is not functioning.
Ollama is not reachable from Hermes.
Why did this not route to the local worker?
Jobs are stuck in the queue.
Show me the last worker heartbeat.
negativeExamples: Rewrite this email to my manager.
Score this GRC internship for me.
What should HR do with an I-9 receipt?

evaluationPrompts.input: My local agent is not functioning.
Ollama is not reachable from Hermes.
Why did this not route to the local worker?
Jobs are stuck in the queue.
The worker still is not claiming it.
evaluationPrompts.reason: Ambiguous worker diagnostic; ask which task or build failed.
Ambiguous build diagnostic should ask for task/project context.
Deployment status belongs to build validation/deployment readiness.

evaluationPrompts.input: Rewrite this email to my manager.
evaluationPrompts.reason: Writing request should not route to local-worker-status.

evaluationPrompts.input: Score this GRC internship for me.
evaluationPrompts.reason: Career/GRC request should not route to local-worker-status.
